Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===

Suite result: 4/4 PASS

Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

input.content:
Project Helios is currently on track for the scheduled product demo to the Meridian Group on July 25, though we are not fully ready to demo as of today. Core development has made solid progress, with the API gateway 100% complete, the data ingestion module 85% complete, and the client dashboard at 60%. However, because Meridian's Project Sponsor, James Whitfield, explicitly emphasized that the demo must feature live data ingestion rather than mock data, several critical blockers must be resolved before the presentation.

The most pressing technical issues involve the data ingestion pipeline, which currently has two high-severity defects identified in Sprint 14. First, date normalization fails for the MM/DD/YYYY format, which affects approximately 40% of Meridian's source data (HEL-D-014). Meridian is slated to provide a clean sample dataset by July 15 to help unblock this. Second, large file ingestion (over 200k records) causes memory overflow and pipeline crashes (HEL-D-015). Given that Meridian has already raised concerns about dashboard performance and load times on large datasets, the engineering team must resolve these crashes and complete the pending load time benchmarks (TC-604 / HEL-247) to ensure a smooth live demonstration.

Beyond the technical defects, there are a few administrative and environmental dependencies to clear. The team needs to secure the license renewal for the GeoSync v2.1 mapping library, which is currently pending legal review and targeted for resolution by July 14. Additionally, we are awaiting an IT infrastructure diagram from Meridian to ensure our integration environment is properly set up for the demo and the subsequent UAT phase.

In summary, while the project foundation is strong, readiness for the demo hinges on resolving the date formatting bug and memory crashes, securing the GeoSync license, and successfully benchmarking the dashboard's performance on large datasets. Addressing these prerequisites will ensure we meet Meridian's expectations for a live data demo and allow us to finalize the demo agenda due to the client on July 18.

content.text:
I have reviewed the relevant project files and written the assessment based on the current status of Project Helios and the expectations of the Meridian Group. The 4-paragraph assessment details our progress, the technical and administrative blockers that need to be resolved before the demo, and the next steps.

The document has been successfully saved to your working directory as `demo_readiness_assessment.txt`.
